name: Ontario - OBCA
description: Ontario business corporation guide tracks for OBR filings, corporate records, securities registers, and beneficial ownership records.

rules.summary: Ontario OBCA annual workflows include annual shareholders meeting timing and financial statements placed before the annual meeting.
rules.tooltipText: Use this for Ontario AGM timing and financial-document presentation checks, then track OBR filings separately.
rules.caveatText: Ontario annual return/profile filings are registry workflows and should be checked against Ontario Business Registry instructions.

rules.summary: Ontario corporations maintain corporate records, including articles, by-laws, minutes/resolutions, securities registers, and significant-control information.
rules.tooltipText: Use this citation track for Ontario minute-book completeness, securities register support, beneficial-ownership review, and registered/head-office record storage.
rules.caveatText: Some records are inspected under different access rules, and Ontario land ownership records may require separate tracking.

rules.summary: Subject to the articles or by-laws, a majority of the directors, or the minimum number required by the articles, constitutes directors' quorum; if fewer than three directors, all directors must be present.
rules.tooltipText: Use the articles and by-laws first, then apply the OBCA directors' quorum default and the all-directors rule for boards under three.
rules.caveatText: Vacancies and articles/by-laws can affect whether remaining directors may act.

rules.summary: An OBCA special resolution generally requires at least two-thirds of votes cast, or written consent by every shareholder entitled to vote.
rules.tooltipText: Use this for Ontario special-resolution vote-threshold checks and unanimous written-consent checks.
rules.caveatText: Some OBCA actions require additional class, article, by-law, or dissent-right analysis.

rules.sectionLabel: Initial Return / Notice of Change / Annual Return
rules.summary: Ontario corporations use the Ontario Business Registry for initial returns, notices of change, annual returns, and articles amendments.
rules.tooltipText: Track the Ontario company key, profile accuracy, initial-return status, annual returns, notices of change, and filing confirmations as registry-maintenance records.
rules.caveatText: Ontario registry filing deadlines and available forms can depend on entity type and filing history.

rules.summary: Ontario profile reports expose registry identity and status fields that can be archived as source evidence for the organization profile.
rules.tooltipText: Use profile reports as dated evidence for corporation number, name, status, registered/head office, directors/officers where available, and extra-provincial limitations.
rules.caveatText: Extra-provincial Ontario profile reports may contain less director/officer detail than the home jurisdiction record.
